---
title: Overview
description: The skeleton is one thing. The look is a theme. react-ui-os ships three reference themes (neutral baseline, cinematic Mintables, SaaS light) and you can write your own.
sidebar:
  order: 1
---

import TypeTable from "~/components/TypeTable.astro";

The library splits "the system" from "the look." The **window manager**, the **dock**, the **menu bar**, the **windowing rules**, the **keyboard shortcuts**: all of that is the skeleton. **Themes** are token bags that dress it.

## Two ways themes flex

| Layer          | What changes                                                                                                      |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Build-time** | The consumer picks one theme at app boot. `<Desktop theme={mintables} />` vs. `<Desktop theme={defaultTheme} />`. |
| **Runtime**    | The end user tweaks a bounded subset via the Settings panel. The theme declares which tokens are open.            |

Runtime themes are not full theme swaps. They are **overlays**: user prefs sit on top of the declared theme's defaults, and only the paths the theme listed under `customizable` are applied. Stale prefs (a token the theme later removed) are silently ignored.

## `OsTheme` shape

<TypeTable name="OsTheme" hideDescription />

Six token categories plus an optional **customizable** map. The library's components consume tokens via `useTheme()`.

## What each category covers

| Category    | Drives                                                                                                     |
| ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `palette`   | Surface colors, text colors, baseline accent, hairline borders. App accents override the baseline per-app. |
| `shape`     | Radii: `windowRadius` (large), `dockTileRadius` (squircle), `small` (pills, chips, tooltips).              |
| `motion`    | Durations in milliseconds and easings as CSS strings. Window open, dock hover, minimize genie.             |
| `blur`      | `backdrop-filter` strings for the menu bar / dock surface and for Spotlight separately.                    |
| `wallpaper` | `src` (image URL), `parallax` (cursor-driven shift), `vignette` (dark radial overlay).                     |
| `chrome`    | Structural variants: window control style, dock position, menu-bar placement.                              |

## `chrome`: the structural lever

<TypeTable name="OsThemeChrome" hideDescription />

This is the part that does not exist in component libraries. It is what lets a SaaS dashboard hide the wallpaper and put the dock on the left, and lets a maker tool keep the full macOS register. Same components, different stance.

## Themes that ship

| Package                        | Stance                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `@react-ui-os/theme-default`   | Unbranded baseline. Dark, neutral palette, no wallpaper, full macOS chrome. Use when you want to feel the skeleton.                            |
| `@react-ui-os/theme-mintables` | Cinematic frosted glass. Deep blur, teal accent, parallax + vignette wallpaper, traffic lights.                                                |
| `@react-ui-os/theme-saas`      | Neutral light workspace. Dock on the left, menu bar hidden, modest blur. Exercises `chrome.dockPosition: "left"` and `chrome.menuBar: "none"`. |

## Picking a theme

```tsx
import { defaultTheme } from "@react-ui-os/theme-default";
import { createMintablesTheme } from "@react-ui-os/theme-mintables";
import { createSaasTheme } from "@react-ui-os/theme-saas";

// Build-time choice. Swap the right side for whichever stance fits.
const theme = createMintablesTheme({ wallpaperSrc: "/wallpaper.jpg" });

<Desktop apps={apps} theme={theme} />;
```

## See also

- [Customizable schema](/themes/customizable/): how a theme exposes runtime overrides to Settings.
- [Writing a theme](/themes/writing/): `createOsTheme` patterns.
- [`useTheme`](/api/usetheme/): reading tokens from a component.
